rag_rules: Use assign only for implementation-level output features or declared auxiliary variables; avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope.
Ensure AGREE contract elements are placed in valid component type scopes for ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings after subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: When AGREE diagnostics indicate type mismatches, align helper declaration types with expression types (bool, int, real).
For boolean expressions in contracts, use standard comparison operators with proper type alignment.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are declared or accessible in the current scope.
Minimize repairs to only the elements causing validation errors without affecting unrelated architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE const, eq, and gua declarations must follow specific type and scope rules.
Implementation contracts should not include guarantee statements; use assign for output bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use component type scope for port-level contracts.
Avoid mismatched types in AGREE expressions; ensure helper declarations match the type of the right-hand side.
